title.en: Table of Ancient and Modern Persons

sentences.zh: 自書契之作，先民可得而聞者，經傳所稱，唐虞以上，帝王有號諡。

translations.literal: Since the making of written records, what can be heard of the men of old, as named in the classics and commentaries, from Tang and Yu upward, is that emperors and kings had titles and posthumous names.
translations.idiomatic: From the time writing was first made, what we can still know of the ancients through the Classics and their commentaries is this: from the ages of Tang and Yu onward, emperors and kings bore formal titles and posthumous names.

sentences.zh: 輔佐不可得而稱矣，而諸子頗言之，雖不考虖孔氏，然猶著在篇籍，歸乎顯善昭惡，勸戒後人，故博采焉。

translations.literal: As for those who assisted and supported them, they cannot be fully named; yet the various masters speak of them somewhat. Though not all can be verified by the Confucian school, they are still recorded in written texts, and all return to making good manifest, making evil clear, exhorting and warning later people, therefore they are broadly collected here.
translations.idiomatic: The ministers and aides who supported those rulers cannot all be fully identified. Still, the various philosophical writings discuss many of them. Even where these accounts cannot be fully confirmed by the Confucian tradition, they remain preserved in the texts and serve a common purpose: to display virtue, expose wrongdoing, and admonish later generations. For that reason, I have gathered them widely.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「若聖與仁，則吾豈敢？」

translations.literal: Confucius said: "As for being sage and benevolent, how would I dare?"
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "As for claiming sagehood and perfect benevolence, how could I dare?"

sentences.zh: 又曰：「何事於仁，必也聖乎！」

translations.literal: He also said: "How could one stop at benevolence? It would have to be sagehood!"
translations.idiomatic: He also said, "Why speak only of benevolence? One would have to reach sagehood."

translations.literal: "If one does not yet know, how can one attain benevolence?"
translations.idiomatic: "If one does not truly understand, how could one possess benevolence?"

translations.literal: "Those who know by birth are highest;
translations.idiomatic: "Those born already knowing stand at the top;

translations.literal: those who learn and then know are next;
translations.idiomatic: those who come to knowledge through study are next;

translations.literal: those who are troubled and then learn are again next;
translations.idiomatic: those who are driven by hardship to learn come after them;

translations.literal: those who are troubled and yet do not learn - among the people, these are the lowest."
translations.idiomatic: and those who suffer hardship yet refuse to learn are the lowest of all."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「中人以上，可以語上也。」

translations.literal: He also said: "With those of middle level and above, one may speak of higher things."
translations.idiomatic: He also said, "With people of average ability and above, one can discuss what is higher."

translations.literal: "Only the highest wisdom and the lowest foolishness do not change."
translations.idiomatic: "Only the supremely wise and the utterly foolish remain unmoved."

sentences.zh: 傳曰：譬如堯舜，禹、稷、镨與之為善則行，鯀、讙兜欲與為惡則誅。

translations.literal: The Tradition says: For example, with Yao and Shun, when Yu, Ji, and Pu acted with them in doing good, it was carried out; when Gun and Huan Dou wished to do evil with them, they were executed.
translations.idiomatic: As the Tradition says: take Yao and Shun. When Yu, Ji, and Pu joined them in doing what was right, those policies were put into effect; when Gun and Huan Dou sought to join them in wrongdoing, they were punished to death.

sentences.zh: 可與為善，不可與為惡，是謂上智。

translations.literal: One can act in goodness with them, but cannot act in evil with them - this is called highest wisdom.
translations.idiomatic: To be able to cooperate in good but never in evil - that is what is called highest wisdom.

sentences.zh: 桀紂，龍逢、比干欲與之為善則誅，于莘、崇侯與之為惡則行。

translations.literal: As for Jie and Zhou, when Long Feng and Bi Gan wished to do good with them, they were executed; when Yuxin and Marquis Chong did evil with them, it was carried out.
translations.idiomatic: By contrast, with Jie and Zhou, Long Feng and Bi Gan were executed for trying to do what was right with them, while Yuxin and the Marquis of Chong were followed when they proposed evil.

sentences.zh: 可與為惡，不可與為善，是謂下愚。

translations.literal: One can act in evil with them, but cannot act in goodness with them - this is called lowest foolishness.
translations.idiomatic: To be capable only of joining in evil, never in good - that is what is called utter folly.

sentences.zh: 齊桓公，管仲相之則霸，豎貂輔之則亂。

translations.literal: As for Duke Huan of Qi, when Guan Zhong assisted him, he became hegemon; when Shu Diao aided him, there was disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan of Qi became hegemon when advised by Guan Zhong, but fell into disorder when guided by Shu Diao.

sentences.zh: 可與為善，可與為惡，是謂中人。

translations.literal: One can act in goodness with them, and can act in evil with them - this is called the middle type of people.
translations.idiomatic: Those who can join either good or evil are what we call people of the middle rank.

sentences.zh: 因茲以列九等之序，究極經傳，繼世相次，總備古今之略要云。

translations.literal: Accordingly, based on this, the order of nine grades is arranged, the classics and commentaries are exhaustively examined, generations are linked in sequence, and in sum the essential outline of antiquity and the present is prepared.
translations.idiomatic: On this basis, I set out a sequence of nine grades, tracing the classics and commentaries as far as possible, arranging each generation in order, and providing in sum a concise outline of notable figures from ancient to later times.

translations.literal: Upper-upper: sages.
translations.idiomatic: Highest grade: sages.

translations.literal: Upper-middle: benevolent men.
translations.idiomatic: Second highest grade: men of benevolence.

translations.literal: Upper-lower: wise men.
translations.idiomatic: Third grade: men of wisdom.

translations.literal: Middle-upper.
translations.idiomatic: Middle grade, upper tier.

translations.literal: Middle-middle.
translations.idiomatic: Middle grade, middle tier.

translations.literal: Middle-lower.
translations.idiomatic: Middle grade, lower tier.

translations.literal: Lower-upper.
translations.idiomatic: Lower grade, upper tier.

translations.literal: Lower-middle.
translations.idiomatic: Lower grade, middle tier.

translations.literal: Lower-lower: foolish men.
translations.idiomatic: Lowest grade: the foolish.

cells.literal: tai hao di mi xi shi
cells.idiomatic: tai hao di mi xi shi.

cells.literal: shao hao di jin tian shi
cells.idiomatic: shao hao di jin tian shi.

cells.literal: zhuan xu di gao yang shi
cells.idiomatic: zhuan xu di gao yang shi.
